concept: Shame / Feeling Small Before Others / The Inward Fold
description: Shame: the feeling of being seen wrongly, or seen truly in a moment one wishes were hidden. Named without judgment — shame felt is human; shame unspeakable curdles. Its healers are 'phamo' (console) and 'thekao' (acknowledge); its cousin 'thiku nila' (seeing-as-small) names shame inflicted from outside.
sound_symbolism: 'nu' folds inward, 'pi' is the smallness felt, 'ra' the open exposure that caused it — the whole event, inside to outside.
grammatical_notes: Noun with 'phaelo': 'mia nupira phaelo.' (I feel shame.) Released: 'shia nupira to leiro.' (They released the shame.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Shame observed with compassion dissolves; shame hidden compounds. The word exists to give the observing somewhere to stand.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Communities that can speak shame can repair without humiliation — the named feeling is the alternative to the defended one.

tags.community: What honest repair must pass through
tags.emotion: The inward fold of being seen
